extraction.one_line_summary: DE GENDT Thomas won Stage 1 from a breakaway by over 3 minutes, taking race lead, while MATTHEWS Michael won bunch sprint for second place.
tactical_patterns: DE GENDT Thomas sustained 4h14m solo breakaway with 3+ minute advantage, signaling peloton's GC conservatism on opening stage.
MATTHEWS Michael contested sprint despite 13+ minute deficit to breakaway, revealing teams prioritized positioning over chase organization.
FROOME Chris crash with still 900m remaining left him outside time cut considerations, illustrating danger of early-stage positioning errors.

rider_signals.DE GENDT Thomas: positive: fourth Catalunya stage win confirms his ability to exploit tactical disinterest on hard stages
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: won bunch sprint comfortably, showing early-season form despite conceding stage to breakaway
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: contested sprint but clearly waiting for selective stages rather than committing early
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: crashed with 900m to go and finished 13+ minutes down, already compromised before mountain stages
rider_signals.MCCARTHY Jay: negative: involved in same late crash as Froome, hitting kerb at critical positioning moment
rider_signals.SCHILLINGER Andreas: negative: caught in late crash, losing time and damaging GC prospects on flat stage
